headline: China Becomes Biggest Enemy Of US AI
summary: OpenAI and Anthropic may want to get their companies public as soon as possible. Microsoft and Meta may want to close financial deals for data centers sometime this year. Several sources say a new AI model from China has already caught up to US AI models across several features. The model in question is GLM-5.2 ... China Becomes Biggest Enemy Of US AI

headline: Microsoft joins AI-driven tech layoff wave with 4,800 job cuts
summary: Microsoft is cutting about 2.1% of its workforce, or roughly 4,800 jobs, as the Windows maker restructures parts of its commercial and Xbox businesses, joining other tech titans in a wave of layoffs as ‌they shift investments towards AI infrastructure.  Big Tech's historic AI outlays, set ‌to top $700 billion this year, are piling pressure on companies to show returns from the technology and offset the rising cost of rolling it out across their ​businesses.  Microsoft announced the cuts on Monday following a nearly 23% slump in its shares in the first six months of 2026, their worst first-half performance since ⁠2022.

content.title: Microsoft joins AI-driven tech layoff wave with 4,800 job cuts
content.summary: Microsoft is cutting about 2.1% of its workforce, or roughly 4,800 jobs, as the Windows maker restructures parts of its commercial and Xbox businesses, joining other tech titans in a wave of layoffs as ‌they shift investments towards AI infrastructure.  Big Tech's historic AI outlays, set ‌to top $700 billion this year, are piling pressure on companies to show returns from the technology and offset the rising cost of rolling it out across their ​businesses.  Microsoft announced the cuts on Monday following a nearly 23% slump in its shares in the first six months of 2026, their worst first-half performance since ⁠2022.

thumbnail.caption: A Microsoft logo is seen in Issy-les-Moulineaux near Paris

headline: AI investors may pivot to hyperscalers from chipmakers, Morgan Stanley says
summary: Morgan Stanley said the recent weakness in U.S. semiconductor stocks is a sign that the market ‌gains are broadening, with investors likely to turn toward ‌AI "hyperscalers" as well as consumer discretionary, transport and biotechnology shares.  In a note dated ​Monday, the brokerage said hyperscalers — an industry term for tech companies that are spending big on data centers — could benefit from a rotation away from semiconductor stocks as the AI cycle shifts.  Although the ‌likes of Alphabet ⁠and Amazon have committed billions to scale up their AI infrastructure, skyrocketing the share prices of semiconductor ⁠companies, clear evidence that AI products can generate returns that justify the spending is yet to be seen.

content.title: AI investors may pivot to hyperscalers from chipmakers, Morgan Stanley says
content.summary: Morgan Stanley said the recent weakness in U.S. semiconductor stocks is a sign that the market ‌gains are broadening, with investors likely to turn toward ‌AI "hyperscalers" as well as consumer discretionary, transport and biotechnology shares.  In a note dated ​Monday, the brokerage said hyperscalers — an industry term for tech companies that are spending big on data centers — could benefit from a rotation away from semiconductor stocks as the AI cycle shifts.  Although the ‌likes of Alphabet ⁠and Amazon have committed billions to scale up their AI infrastructure, skyrocketing the share prices of semiconductor ⁠companies, clear evidence that AI products can generate returns that justify the spending is yet to be seen.

headline: Bespoke Labs Announces $40M to Build the Environments That Train Reliable Agents
summary: MOUNTAIN VIEW, Calif., July 06, 2026--Bespoke Labs, an AI agent training platform, today announced that it has raised $40 million across a Series A round led by Wing VC, with participation from Mayfield, The House Fund, dbt Labs CEO Tristan Handy, and other angels from Anthropic, OpenAI, and Meta, as well as a seed round led by 8VC, with participation from Jeff Dean, Resolve AI CEO Spiros Xanthos, DevRev CEO Dheeraj Pandey, and others.
